mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity capital relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=0.1% indicates maximum accommodation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market strength and rising consumer income. Sustained payroll growth over the prior 3 months predicts positive equity returns as higher employment supports consumption and corporate earnings. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: Positive 3-month PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate profit growth. The interaction of these two demand proxies produces stronger predictive power than either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1001.0 thousand units, RSAFS=439891.0 million dollars
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. When INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average, equity investors reduce risk exposure due to deteriorating economic fundamentals, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines > 0.5% fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3% year-over-year triggers expectations of future monetary tightening. Anticipated rate increases compress equity valuations through higher discount rates. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when inflation crosses above the 3% threshold.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3% fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. The interaction between rising unemployment and declining payroll growth (PAYEMS) creates a dual confirmation of labor weakness that predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: Joint condition of UNRATE > 6.5% and negative PAYEMS growth fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly provides the broadest demand signal. When annualized real GDP growth falls below 1.5%, this signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions that compress equity valuations. This always-on trend signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1=15824.2 billions of chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Annualized GDPC1 growth below 1.5% fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both series show sustained growth (3-month trend positive), this indicates robust productive capacity and labor availability that supports corporate profit expansion and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with full capacity utilization
failure_condition: Joint positive 3-month trends in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.
